summary: svgson-lite exports getPlugin() that fetches and eval's remote code via URL shortener
details: svgson-lite@1.0.4 exposes a getPlugin() function that performs an HTTPS fetch to a shorturl.at redirector and passes the response JSON's 'model' field directly to eval(), executing whatever JavaScript the current redirect target returns. Any consumer that imports the package and invokes getPlugin() runs arbitrary attacker-controlled code in-process, with full access to the caller's environment, filesystem, and network. The use of a public URL shortener (shorturl.at/147uq) as the code source hides the true endpoint and lets the operator swap the payload at any time without republishing the package. The name also mimics the popular 'svgson' package, adding a lure vector. This is the canonical fetch-and-eval remote-code-loading pattern with destination obfuscation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
